creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, Jun 09, 2008 at 02:50:49AM +0200, Matthias Andree wrote:
> On Sun, 08 Jun 2008, Wesley Shields wrote:
> 
> > Could you be please more specific as to how this port is broken?  I'd be
> > happy to commit the dependency change, but I'm not sure why it needs to
> > be done.
> 
> Hi Wesley,
> 
> after a pkg_remove gettext (I had 0.16... installed, 0.17 was in ports)
> I tried to install rpm, and for some reason, it failed in the configure
> phase with a "gawk: no such file or directory" (if memory serves me
> right) quite early, and installing gawk fixed that. I tried to debug it,
> and the rpm configure script is supposed to go hunting for mawk gawk
> nawk awk, apparently it failed early. I didn't bother to spend more time
> on that, but just installed gawk and then rpm installed fine.
> 
> Is that what you were hoping for or do you need further info? Feel free
> to ask.

I'm unable to reproduce this given the circumstances listed above.
Given the port does not break in a tinderbox, jail, or anywhere else for
that matter I'd like to close this PR.  Any objections?

-- WXS
